LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING ON PLAINTIFF SECURITY NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR ASSIGNMENT OF RIGHTS AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF/JUDGMENT CREDITOR SECURITY NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S CCP SEC. 708.510 MOTION FOR AN "ASSIGNMENT ORDER RE: RIGHTS TO PAYMENT OF MONEY DUE OR TO BECOME DUE" TO DEFENDANT/DEBTOR MECA BUILDERS INC. IS DENIED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) THE COURT TO PREPARE THE ORDER. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR ASSIGNMENT OF RIGHTS
Off Calendar Aug-23-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance, or objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF SECURITY NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR ASSIGNMENT OF RIGHTS as follows:

Plaintiff/judgment creditor Security National Insurance Company's CCP Sec. 708.510 motion for an "assignment order re: rights to payment of money due or to become due" to defendant/debtor Meca Builders Inc. is DENIED.

Plaintiff broadly seeks assignment of "the interest, if any of" Meca "in its rights to payment of money due or to become due, whether styled accounts receivable, general intangibles, payment intangibles, accounts, deposit accounts, royalties, fees, commissions, and other rights to money, from its activities as a building contractor."

Notwithstanding CCP Sec. 708.510's plain language ("the court may order the judgment debtor to assign"), some courts have ruled that an assignment thereunder may be outright, not simply an order directing the judgment debtor to assign, and the assignment may reach future events. (Ahart, California Practice Guide: Enforcing Judgments and Debts (TRG 2019) Secs. 6:1422.6, 6:1423.)

However, as plaintiff concedes, it is required to describe the sources of the right to payment with sufficient detail so defendant can file a claim of exemption or other opposition. This plaintiff does not do. The most liberal cases it cites - Greenbaum v. Islamic Republic of Iran (2008) 782 F.Supp.2d 893, 897 and Philippine Export & Foreign Loan v. Chuidian (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 1058 - regard arcane issues of international law, and the former specifies five oil companies as sources by name.

The Court to prepare the order.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
